description: BBC Jazz Award-winning tenor saxophonist, with pianist Liam Noble, bassist Oli Hayhurst and drummer Gene Calderazzo — one of the UK's most consistently inventive quartets. Album *Vista* made Jazzwise's top-20 of the year.

Venezuelan pianist and composer who brings rare harmonic depth to standards and originals alike. Based between New York and Caracas, Gonzalez plays with the kind of melodic clarity that recalls McCoy Tyner at his most lyrical.
Royal Academy graduates.
Tomorrow's Warriors quartet led by drummer Yuma Angius-Thomas, Young Drummer of the Year 2024 finalist. Originals and arrangements drawing on Snarky Puppy, Brecker and Cory Wong.
Hardcore European free improv. Three sets across different configurations, anchored by Smyth's long-standing trio with bassist John Edwards and drummer Mark Sanders.

artist_name: Billy Cobham with the Guy Barker Big Band
description: The drummer who defined fusion — Cobham played on Miles Davis's Bitches Brew sessions and led the legendary Mahavishnu Orchestra. Joining him is Guy Barker's Big Band for a rare collision of jazz-rock power and orchestral swing.

Award-winning French pianist performing music from his fourth album *Resurgence* with double bassist Damian Nueva, drummer Arnaud Dolmen and percussionist Abraham Mansfarroll. LetterOne Rising Stars Jazz Award 2018 winner — Afro-Latin rhythms, blues, funk and French impressionism in the mix.

artist_name: Women in Jazz Media Presents: Jasna Jovićević
description: Serbian sax player's band.
Album launch for Rob Cope's Joyride at Crazy Coqs.

artist_name: Sarah Moule & Simon Wallace — Songs For Scarlet Women
description: Sophisticated jazz cabaret celebrating the femme fatale — songs by pianist Simon Wallace and lyricist Fran Landesman, sung by Sarah Moule. Boston Room at Chiswick's George IV.

Pianist-composer Kate Williams performs music from her new album 'On the Wing of a Dragonfly' alongside jazz standards. A Parliamentary Jazz Award winner playing the intimate Culford Room at Cadogan Hall.

London's leading hard-bop tenor man, who hosts the Late Late Show at Ronnie's. Debut album *The Chase* was in The Times' top-10 jazz of 2017 and the first jazz debut to get a Guardian 5-star. High-octane, Coltrane and Henderson at the root.

Gypsy swing doesn't get more transportive than this — Djangada's Hot Club-influenced sound conjures smoky Parisian cafés with a rawness that studio recordings never quite capture. Sam Watts' virtuosic guitar work has drawn comparisons to Django Reinhardt himself, and hearing it live in the intimate surrounds of Ladbroke Hall is a genuine privilege.

Soulful piano-vocals don't get much more visceral than this — Natalie Duncan's voice has been compared to Nina Simone's, and that's not hype. Catching her in the intimate surrounds of Ronnie Scott's legendary Frith Street room is the kind of Friday night you'll be talking about on Saturday.

São Paulo-born bassist launching debut solo album *Maria* (Far Out Recordings) — a homecoming record drawing on samba, Baião and Maracatu. Collaborators include Emily Sandé, Nile Rodgers, Janelle Monáe, Gregory Porter and Azymuth. Ten-piece band. Both shows sold out.
London collective featuring Tori Freestone on sax.
First rate Royal Academy of Music graduate — bassist, composer, bandleader. Guardian named him 'one of the UK's most acclaimed young bandleaders'. Album *Effra* (2025) on Edition Records.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: One of London's most deeply schooled bebop pianists — fluent in the tradition from Monk and Powell through to Bill Evans and Herbie Hancock. Regular at the Soho venue that has always been his natural home.

One of British jazz's most restless figures — founder of Sons of Kemet, The Comet Is Coming and Shabaka and the Ancestors — returns to the saxophone in 2026 following his flute-focused solo album Perceive Its Beauty, Acknowledge Its Grace. A one-off collaborative performance, part of Harry Styles' Meltdown.

Three generations of British improvisers lock in for a rare one-off, with guitar legend Jim Mullen — whose playing graced Van Morrison's classic *Wavelength* album — anchoring a set that crackles with hard-won chemistry. Catch this before the moment passes; lineups like this don't get repeated.
Saxophonist and bandleader who has played alongside Cory Wong, Ed Sheeran, John Legend, Mariah Carey and Joss Stone.
North London-born vocalist known for gospel-infused power and a striking soprano range.
An Evening of Jazz & Soul Divas — Katriona Taylor's A-list quintet with specially arranged numbers from Amy Winehouse, Billie Holiday, Alicia Keys and Etta James.
The LA saxophonist whose 2015 triple album The Epic announced a new era for spiritual jazz pays tribute to John Coltrane and Miles Davis. Afternoon show, part of Harry Styles' Meltdown.

Evening show performing his 2024 album Fearless Movement in full — sweeping, full-ensemble spiritual jazz with cinematic scope. Second of two Royal Festival Hall shows on the same day. Part of Harry Styles' Meltdown.
Legendary jazz pianist, singer-songwriter and producer bringing six decades of musical mastery — a Bob Dylan collaborator and architect of the sophisticated West Coast jazz-pop sound.

Drummer Storey leads one of London's tightest creative trios alongside saxophonist James Allsopp and bassist Conor Chaplin. Third album *Circeo* (2023) confirmed their standing — quicksilver interplay built on years of touring, tradition worn lightly.
London septet playing original compositions — a larger ensemble with room for the kind of collective interplay that a quartet doesn't afford.
Out to Lunch series at Cadogan Hall's Culford Room — Sarah L King Quartet with Barry Green, Jakub Cywinski and Eric Ford.

artist_name: Raffy Bushman – New Life Album Launch
description: Debut album *New Life* — piano trio rooted in jazz and hip-hop, with syncopated grooves and Motown warmth — out on Bridge The Gap just weeks before this Vortex launch. BBC Radio 6 Music, Radio 2, Worldwide FM and Jazz FM have all backed it. 57k Spotify monthly listeners and climbing.

Six-time Grammy nominee whose album 'Phoenix' brought three Grammy nods and Alto Saxophonist of the Year from the Jazz Journalists Association. Her live performances blend jazz, funk, R&B and hip-hop with fearless improvisation.
Tasteful sax-led band.

artist_name: Julian Nicholas, Emil Viklicky and Imogen Ryall
description: Czech pianist joins UK pros.

The father of Ethio-jazz — the Ethiopian composer who fused jazz, Latin and traditional Ethiopian music into a singular sound during the Swinging Addis era of the late 1960s, later brought to new audiences via Jim Jarmusch's 2005 film Broken Flowers. Part of Harry Styles' Meltdown.

Catch this London-based collective on a rare hometown night, bringing the kind of loose-limbed improvisation that's turned heads at festivals across Europe. Four musicians who genuinely listen to each other — the result feels spontaneous every time, because it largely is.

artist_name: Sun-Mi Hong’s NOCTILUME feat. Mette Rasmussen
description: Korean-Norwegian drummer Sun-Mi Hong brings her luminous NOCTILUME project to the Vortex, joined by fire-breathing saxophonist Mette Rasmussen — one of Europe's most talked-about improvisers right now. Expect something genuinely unpredictable: rhythmic architecture that breathes, and a front-line presence that'll make the room feel smaller than it is.

artist_name: KAIYO presents Jazz Stories with Malcolm Earle Smith
description: Vocalist Malcolm Earle Smith with the KAIYO collective.
Steel pan virtuoso Mark Cherrie's second quartet album showcases the instrument as a serious jazz medium. Unexpected cover versions alongside original compositions.

Pianist who recorded with The Cinematic Orchestra and regularly tours with Mulatu Astatke, now leading his own quintet with Pete Wareham, Byron Wallen, Tom Herbert and Corrie Dick. Rooted in Miles and Coltrane but folding in afrobeat, Latin and free moments. Jazzwise called him 'a musician worth keeping a very close eye on'.

Few guitarists in the London scene blend West African rhythms with bebop vocabulary as naturally as Karl Onibuje, making every gig feel like a genuine musical conversation across continents. Ladbroke Hall's intimate setting means you'll hear every nuance of his intricate fretwork up close.

British-Ghanaian alto saxophonist who cut his teeth in the Jazz Warriors alongside Gary Crosby, then played with Donald Byrd, Abdullah Ibrahim, Andrew Hill Big Band and Ornette Coleman. Hard-bop fluency with a deep grounding in Monk — he co-founded the Monk Liberation Front with Jonathan Gee.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Lunchtime jazz at MaMaSaint, 210 Trinity Road SW17. Karen Sharp (saxophone) and Paul Edis (piano). 12:30pm.
Jazz funk six-piece featuring vocalist Stuey Love (The New Jazz Mags) with Volker Jansson, Paul Stead, Mark Buckingham, Tom Gilmour and Theo Buckingham.
scraper_notes: UK Jazz News newsletter 9 Jun 2026 | hidden: amateur band, not suitable for listings

artist_name: David Austin Grey – A Hologram Maze
description: Dreamy, shape-shifting music that sits somewhere between late-night soul and cosmic improvisation — this is the kind of set that makes the Vortex feel like the only room in London. Grey's debut album turned serious heads in 2024, and catching him in a room this intimate won't be an option forever.

South London drummer whose 2023 debut album Black Classical Music — recorded live at Abbey Road — became one of the most celebrated British jazz records in years. Intense, rhythmically complex, deeply soulful. Part of Harry Styles' Meltdown.

artist_name: MOPOMOSO: International Free Improvised Music – June 2026

description: Sarah L King explores romance and humour through jazz standards in the Boston Room at Chiswick's George IV.
Windrush Day celebration led by Ayo Vincent.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Royal Academy sax graduate.
Vocalist and composer Evelyn Axton brings a string-augmented ensemble to the Vortex — a format this room rarely hosts, which makes it worth catching.
Cape Town-born pianist blending calypso, Afrobeat, gospel and jazz — his album 'Umoya' features top UK musicians in vibrant, rhythm-rich compositions.

Scottish pianist hailed as one of the most significant new voices in European jazz. His trio albums on Edition Records have drawn comparisons to Keith Jarrett's ECM work. A solo set is a rare and intimate experience.

artist_name: Simon Woolf's Jazz Cello Project ft Gareth Lockrane
description: Cellist Simon Woolf brings his jazz cello project to the 606, joined by flautist Gareth Lockrane. One of a small handful of jazz cellists active in London — an instrument pairing you won't hear often.
Series promoting recent jazz school graduates.
The French horn almost never fronts a small jazz group — Rattigan makes a compelling case that it should. Quintet format with rhythm section.

Five decades of music united in one refined concert experience. Di Meola's acoustic band plays 'The Guitarchitect' — sweeping multimedia visuals, the stories behind a lifetime of artistry. One of the most technically brilliant guitarists alive, still at the peak of his powers.
Youth big band.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Tasteful sax-led band.
Album launch — husband and wife tough tenor combo Dave O'Higgins and forensic pathologist Dr Judith O'Higgins.
Alto saxophonist leading a London-based group that blends modern swing, soul and global influences. Forward-looking music rooted in tradition.

Four heavyweights in an intimate Dalston room — this is the kind of spontaneous, no-safety-net improvisation that reminds you why live music exists. Drummer Mark Holub anchors a fearless collective that's equally at home dismantling a standard as building something entirely new from scratch.
London-based guitarist and bandleader Vaughan Hawthorne-Nelson brings his quartet to the Vortex for an evening of original compositions.
French vocalist who became a YouTube sensation with her scat improvisation over Coltrane's Giant Steps. Live she's electrifying — her voice an instrument of extraordinary range and wit.

Soulful grooves and improvised fire collide as this boundary-pushing collective takes over World Heart Beat's intimate Nine Elms space. Expect a tight, brass-heavy sound with deep rhythmic roots and the kind of electric ensemble chemistry that makes Friday nights worth staying out for.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Catch one of London's most lyrical saxophonists doing what he does best — spinning melodies that feel inevitable, like they couldn't have gone any other way. Jones has shared stages with the likes of Wynton Marsalis, and on a Saturday night at the Vortex, that pedigree fills every corner of the room.
South London guitarist and bandleader Mike de Souza at PizzaExpress Jazz Club — groove-based originals drawing on jazz, funk and afrobeat.

Led Bib are one of the UK's most reliably abrasive large-group improvisers — Mercury Prize-nominated, two-saxophone-led, no prisoners. Paired with Holly Reinhardt's sextet at the Cockpit: the experimental end of the spectrum.
Rob Luft (guitar) — toured with Django Bates, recorded with Polar Bear — joins Danish bassist Anne Lukassen and drummer Jonas Seemann for a rare trio outing.

Kaidi Akinnibi — saxophonist and collaborator on Tom Misch & Yussef Dayes' What Kinda Music, and Celeste's chart-topping Not Your Muse — leads this quartet with pianist Finn Carter. Shaped by Tomorrow's Warriors and Kinetika Bloco, his genre-bending sound has also taken him through Black Midi, Wizkid and DoomCannon.
Grammy-nominated vocalist and pianist — her album 'How Love Begins' was recorded with Dan Pugach and Idan Morim.

artist_name: Clark Tracey's Tribute to Stan Tracey
description: Clark Tracey leads original band members Simon Allen and Andrew Cleyndert — with Gareth Williams — in celebrating the 50-year legacy of his father Stan Tracey, the 'godfather of British jazz'.

New York-born pianist making his London bandleading debut, praised by The Wire and UK Jazz News for a 'refreshing and virtuosic lyricism'. Shaped by stints with Tyshawn Sorey and Melissa Aldana, his 2025 debut album 'Canopy' drew acclaim from DownBeat, JazzTimes and the New York Times.
Anglo-French duo combining British and Francophone jazz sensibilities. Understated, chamber-scale improvisation in the right room for it.

Catch this rising London quartet on one of their rare hometown dates – they've spent most of 2026 tearing up festivals across Europe. The Vortex's intimate room means you'll feel every conversation these four musicians have with each other.

LA producer and pianist Kiefer Shackelford bridges hip-hop and acoustic jazz — his albums on Stones Throw Records have drawn praise from Pitchfork and earned a devoted following on both sides of the Atlantic.
London-based Swiss pianist, singer and composer whose award-winning album 'Thrive' gained global acclaim. Poetry, folk and storytelling woven into her lyrical quartet sound.

artist_name: Lucy-Anne Daniels: Hanging with Betty Carter
description: Rooted in the vibrant tradition of Betty Carter — the fearless vocalist who once mentored a young Wynton Marsalis — this is a night of daring, conversational singing where no two phrases land quite the same way twice. Catch Lucy-Anne Daniels at Ladbroke Hall before word properly gets out.

artist_name: Coltrane and Hartman on Impulse! feat. Mark Lockheart and Marvin Muoneké
description: One of the most intimate albums ever recorded gets a rare live outing, with saxophonist Mark Lockheart and vocalist Marvin Muoneké breathing new life into a 1963 masterpiece that even non-believers tend to fall for. Catch it at the Vortex before someone tells you they saw it and you didn't.

artist_name: London Jazz Orchestra Vortex Sessions – “Primetime for Henry”
description: The LJO's monthly Vortex residency dedicates this edition to Henry Mancini — composer of *Peter Gunn*, *The Pink Panther* and the scores that defined cool in the early 1960s.

Catch this rising London quartet on the cusp of something big — their shape-shifting sound blends post-bop intensity with electronic textures in a way that feels genuinely new. The Vortex is the perfect room for it: intimate enough that you'll feel every dynamic shift.

event_title: Miles Davis and John Coltrane at 100
supporting_acts: Julian Pollack (keys), Charles Altura (gtr), Oscar Seaton (dms), David DJ Ginyard (bass)
description: Ravi Coltrane — tenor saxophonist and son of John — joins trumpeter Terence Blanchard to mark 100 years since his father's birth. Nobody carries that legacy more personally, and Blanchard, one of the most decorated brass players of his generation, is the ideal partner.
special_occasion: Centenary tribute — Miles Davis & Coltrane at 100

artist_name: Zoe Rahman Trio – Album Launch (2 Shows)
description: British-Bangladeshi pianist and Mercury Prize nominee. Rahman's duo album with her brother is one of the finest jazz recordings of recent years. Two shows in one day signals something special.

Bangalore-born vocalist Varijashree Venugopal blends South Indian classical technique with fearless improvisation in a combination that stops rooms cold. Catch this rare London appearance in the intimate setting of Ladbroke Hall — dinner included, so arrive hungry in every sense.
The 30-piece Down for the Count Swing Orchestra celebrates Nat King Cole — Mona Lisa, Let's Face the Music, Unforgettable and more from one of swing's most iconic voices.

artist_name: De Beauvoir Jazz Festival: Lucy-Anne Daniels & Liselotte Östblom
description: Two Scandinavian-influenced voices collide at one of London's most beloved intimate clubs, where the room's legendary acoustics make every whispered note feel personal. Daniels and Östblom have been turning heads across European festival circuits, and this rare shared billing is the kind of quietly unmissable night you'll wish you'd told more people about.

Catch a rising star before the word fully gets out — Swiss pianist Anatole Muster brings a rare harmonic imagination that makes even seasoned listeners lean forward. He's been turning heads across Europe's festival circuit, and a Vortex residency at this stage of a career usually means something special is about to happen.
Guitarist Sam Dunn with Dave O'Higgins exploring classic Blue Note hard bop — Hammond organ, drums, and lesser-played Grant Green alongside driving swing.

Fierce, shape-shifting improvisation meets cinematic tension when this London-based collective takes the Vortex stage — their debut album landed on multiple best-of-2025 lists for good reason. If you like music that keeps you genuinely unsure what's coming next, this is your Wednesday.

artist_name: Ruth Naomi Floyd Quartet with Julian Joseph
description: Soulful vocals meet piano brilliance as this powerhouse quartet brings warmth and swing to the stunning Embassy Gardens waterfront. Legendary keysman Julian Joseph joins Ruth Naomi Floyd for a summer evening of groove, spirit, and serious musicianship.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Harpist Valeria Clarke, guitarist/producer Peter Michaels and percussionist Marcinho Pereira blending classical, electronica and South American rhythms.

Three heavyweights sharing a stage with no fixed leader means anything can happen — and usually does. Hitchcock's been tearing up European festival circuits while Golub brings a New York sensibility that keeps the whole thing beautifully off-balance.

Colombian-born pianist Damian Nueva brings a rare fluency between Latin rhythms and post-bop sophistication that makes his music feel genuinely alive rather than stylistically boxed-in. Catch his quintet at the intimate Ladbroke Hall before this fast-rising voice becomes a much harder ticket to get.

event_title: Stacey Kent: A Time for Love Tour
supporting_acts: Jim Tomlinson (sax), Art Hirahara (piano), Tom Hubbard (bass)
description: Over a billion streams and a Grammy nomination. Kent's multi-lingual repertoire includes originals co-written by Nobel laureate Kazuo Ishiguro. New album 'A Time for Love' out this summer. Thursday sold out — Friday still available.

Six decades of brilliance collide in one landmark night as this piano legend marks a milestone birthday in spectacular style. Expect a star-studded celebration packed with groove, soul, and the kind of musicianship that has defined London's scene for generations.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

artist_name: Tony Kofi’s Tentet plays Monk at The Town Hall
description: Alto and soprano saxophone master who survived a near-death experience that led him to dedicate his life to music — a story featured on BBC Radio. His Tentet plays Monk at The Town Hall: Thelonious Sphere Monk's most ambitious compositions, reimagined with ten musicians.

supporting_acts: Chris Fishman (piano/keys), Jermaine Paul (bass), Joe Dyson (drums)
description: Twenty Grammy wins. Metheny brings Side-Eye III+ to the Barbican — new music alongside reimagined classics, mentoring the next generation while exploring fresh sonic territory.

supporting_acts: Chris Fishman (piano/keys), Jermaine Paul (bass), Joe Dyson (drums)
description: Sunday matinee — one of three Barbican shows across the weekend.

artist_name: Sam Eastmond & The Composers Workshop Big Band
description: Full big band under Sam Eastmond's direction — a format the Vortex rarely hosts.

Catch this young pianist before the rest of London figures out how good he is — his trio turns intricate originals into something that feels both urgent and inevitable. Goldmark studied with some of the heaviest names at the Royal Academy, and that pedigree shows in every carefully weighted note.

Daughter of guitarist John Williams, she's been a quietly distinctive force since winning the John Dankworth Award. Her sixth album 'On the Wing of a Dragonfly' — out May 2026, six years in the making — draws Bill Evans comparisons for its patient, spacious trio writing, with Monk-ian moments and a bop-flavoured directness that Jazzwise praised for 'lucidity, spacious approach and surprising gift for quirky hooks.'
Quentin Collins (trumpet) leads a one-off five-way collective — four other London voices, no fixed leader.

Catch one of the most kinetic drummers alive on a night that's less concert, more full-body experience. The New Zealand-born, London-based polymath — whose records have been streamed over 50 million times — builds rhythm into something that feels genuinely spiritual.

Ancient Persian mysticism meets soulful improvisation as this magnetic vocalist weaves Sufi poetry through blues-soaked grooves. A rare, transcendent night at World Heart Beat's intimate Embassy Gardens stage.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: Sharp, searching saxophone lines meet an iron-tight rhythm section as this rising London quartet road-tests material that's already turning heads on the circuit. Johnson's been picking up serious buzz since his stint with Zara McFarlane's band — catch him here before the venues get bigger.

Hackney's beloved Vortex is the perfect room for Leon Foster Thomas's hypnotic blend of Trinidadian parang rhythms and hard-swinging improvisation — music that genuinely sounds like nowhere else. A Mercury Prize-nominated artist bringing real Caribbean soul to north-east London on a Saturday night.

artist_name: Tomorrow’s Warriors presents BXL x LDN Interplay III
description: Two of Europe's most forward-thinking youth collectives — one Brussels, one London — collide for a third time in what's become one of the most creatively charged cross-city exchanges on the scene. Tomorrow's Warriors is the incubator that launched Nubya Garcia and Shabaka Hutchings, so expect the next wave to arrive loud and restless.

artist_name: Finn Carter with Yves Fernandez and Richard Spaven
description: Drummer Richard Spaven has powered sessions for Flying Lotus and José James, and here he locks in with pianist Finn Carter and bassist Yves Fernandez for what promises to be a masterclass in dynamic interplay. Catch this at the Vortex before word spreads further.
Polish Jazz Festival opening gala — EABS fusing hip-hop, electronics and new jazz, followed by the Wojtek Mazolewski Quintet premiering bold new music. Two of Poland's most vital acts.
One of Poland's most acclaimed artists reimagines her best-known songs in jazz arrangements — originals alongside American and Polish standards.
The legendary Polish vocalist who performed with Komeda, Hancock and Sting brings seven decades of music to Cadogan Hall with her new album UlaNova.

artist_name: Linda May Han Oh & Fabian Almazan
description: Grammy-winning bassist Linda May Han Oh with pianist Fabian Almazan — one of jazz's most inventive contemporary duos.
UK premiere — Mariusz Szypura with Adrian Utley (Portishead) and John Stanier reimagining Chopin through mechanical deconstruction and experimental texture.
Poland's most influential pianist pays tribute to Krzysztof Komeda — composer of Rosemary's Baby and the defining voice of Polish jazz. A rare London appearance.
The 17-piece Pete Long Orchestra plays Ella Fitzgerald, Frank Sinatra, Count Basie, Duke Ellington and Jazz at the Philharmonic — 1956 was a landmark year for jazz.
MacArthur Fellow and one of the most intellectually formidable pianists in jazz — Moran's solo concerts are landmark events.
Catalan trombonist-singer Rita Payés with pianist Lucía Fumero — Mediterranean warmth, modern harmony and understated virtuosity in an intimate evening of jazz and song.
Cohen opens his Wigmore residency with a programme marking 100 years since the births of Miles Davis and John Coltrane — with Philip Norris, Joe Farnsworth, Tivon Pennicott and Benny Benack III.
Mexican-born vibraphonist Patricia Brennan leads her Septet — one of the most original voices in contemporary jazz.

artist_name: Lizz Wright with Kenny Banks Sr.
description: EFG London Jazz Festival. The New York Times called her voice 'a smoky contralto'. Lizz Wright returns after a 20-year career defined by luminous storytelling and soulful grace.

Brazilian vocal sorcery meets lush string arrangements as Luiza Zan weaves between bossa nova, MPB and contemporary improvisation with her Muse Quartet. A rare London appearance from one of Europe's most captivating voices — not to be missed.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

artist_name: Andy Sheppard Trio + Norma Winstone & Kit Downes
description: EFG London Jazz Festival double bill — Andy Sheppard Trio with Rita Marcotulli, followed by the extraordinary Norma Winstone with Kit Downes.

Sudanese-British vocalist Amira Kheir brings her mesmerising blend of East African rhythms and contemporary soul to the intimate World Heart Beat stage in Nine Elms. Expect hypnotic grooves, rich storytelling, and a sound that crosses continents with ease.
scraper_notes: World Heart Beat Jazz in the Gardens 2026

description: EFG London Jazz Festival. Norwegian trumpeter meets Greek pianist in pure improvised dialogue — two artists from opposite musical climates finding organic common ground.
Wigmore Hall Associate Artist Harold López-Nussa — Cuban pianist of rare elegance and rhythmic intelligence — with Brazilian guitarist André Da Silva. Cuba meets Brazil.
BBC Radio 2-championed multi-instrumentalist, debut album *From the Fire* (2023). Second LP *Bodies of Water* due September.
scraper_notes: RAH Late Night Jazz Elgar Room — manually added

description: South London sax-bass-drums trio with electronic textures — Afrobeat, hip-hop, grime and bebop in the mix. Jazz FM and BBC airplay.
scraper_notes: RAH Late Night Jazz Elgar Room — manually added

description: Debut album *The Salted Air* was a Rough Trade Album of the Year — folk, film score and poetry influences. One-off London performance with full band.
scraper_notes: RAH Late Night Jazz Elgar Room — manually added

description: Royal College of Music Junior Department — sixth Late Night Jazz appearance. Swing era to present day.
scraper_notes: RAH Late Night Jazz Elgar Room — manually added
